ops.title: Swept working-copy recovery in jj Advance mutations
ops.body:
# Swept working-copy recovery in jj Advance mutations

`jj new` and `jj rebase` take their own working-copy snapshot at execution time, so any classification made before them (probeWorkingCopy, changedPathsGeneratedOnly) is stale by construction — a TOCTOU that once swept user edits off disk into an orphaned head. Advance therefore treats every mutation as unverified until proven clean, post-hoc.

## Invariant

Advance never ends with user-typed content absent from the working tree, except the OutcomeSwept hands-off mode — which preserves the content in a **visible** commit, issues zero further commands, and is logged loudly by sync. Content on `linguist-generated` paths is exempt by charter: branch B exists to discard it toward trunk, pre-window and mid-window alike. Do not "fix" the path-only subset check into a content check — that was reviewed and rejected deliberately.

## Ownership proof (op-log shape)

Before the mutation, capture the full 128-hex op head. After it, `opsSince` (8-op page, since-op exclusive, newest first) must match the **ordered shape** of the mutation's own ops: newest == `new empty commit` (branch A) or prefix `rebase commit ` (branch B), plus at most one older `snapshot working copy`. Anything else — wrong order, wrong cardinality, since-op off the page — is foreign activity: hands off, OutcomeSwept. Set-membership matching is insufficient (a user's own `jj new` emits an allowlisted string; the shape check catches it — pinned by TestJJAdvanceSweptConcurrentNewHandsOff).

## Detection

- Branch A: `changeSurvived` via `jj log -r 'present(<change-id>)' --ignore-working-copy -T change_id` — a clean `jj new` auto-abandons the empty undescribed @, so ANY survival means the classification went stale (also catches a raced describe/bookmark). Empty stdout = abandoned; exit 0 both ways on jj 0.43.
- Branch B: post-rebase changed and conflicted path sets must be ⊆ the pre-classified generated set.

## Recovery (forward-first, deliberate tradeoff)

Own-ops-only sweeps recover **forward**: rebase the survivor onto trunk with `--ignore-working-copy` (it is not @; IWC avoids snapshotting the fresh empty @), fall back to its original parents when conflicted (conflict algebra cancels on back-rebase — empirically pinned), then `jj edit` it (no IWC — the tree must materialize). Branch B back-rebases @ itself **without** IWC (the tree must follow @). `jj op restore` of the pre-mutation op is a trap: that op's recorded @ never contained the interim dirt — recovery must be content-addressed by change id.

Three degradations to OutcomeSwept, all content-preserving: foreign op in the window; IsWorkingCopyContention on any recovery command (the user is acting — do not fight them); a second sweep landing in the edit's own snapshot window (detected via survival of the interim trunk commit, which auto-abandons only when empty).

## Testing seam

vcstest's ShimJJ/ShimJJDirtOn PATH shim injects working-copy writes at the exact mutating argv — production code carries no seam. Recovery tests double as version-drift canaries: the pinned op-description strings and stderr contracts (`Error: No conflicts found at this revision`, `Concurrent checkout`) fail loudly against a changed jj binary.

Landed in commit 845fb6cc (cc-task b16944e9).
ops.when: when modifying Advance's mutation verification, recovery logic, or operation-log ownership proof
